title: Worcester man receives life sentence for home invasion
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Concepcion Rodriguez, 39, from Worcester sentenced to life for 2022 Fitchburg home invasion, according to prosecutors.
Rodriguez convicted of multiple charges, including armed assault and possession of a firearm during a felony.
Defense attorney Kevin Larson plans to appeal, citing lack of habitual criminal warning and constitutional concerns.

title: Derrick White talks beer, being a Bostonian, and a promising Celtics playoff run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Derrick White talks about adjusting to life in Boston since joining the Celtics in February 2022.
White participated in a Sam Adams event, introducing a line of beers named after Boston sports figures.
The Celtics are second in the Eastern Conference with a 53-25 record as playoffs approach.

title: Mike Krzyzewski threw a subtle jab at Bill Belichick in response to new UNC hire
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: UNC hires former Nuggets coach Michael Malone as men's basketball coach, replacing Hubert Davis.
Mike Krzyzewski comments on UNC's hiring of NFL and NBA champion coaches, including Bill Belichick.
UNC fired Mack Brown after a 6-7 season; Tar Heels went 4-8 under Belichick's first season.

title: Person stabs dog in Milton during alleged attack
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A person allegedly stabbed a dog during an attack in Milton on Blue Hill River Road.
The dog was off-leash and reportedly attacked the person, leading to the stabbing.
State Police and Milton Animal Control are investigating the incident, and the dog is at a local vet.

title: Alex Cora reshuffles Red Sox lineup, drops Trevor Story out of No. 2 spot
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Alex Cora reshuffles Red Sox lineup, moving Trevor Story from No. 2 to No. 5 after a slow start.
Masataka Yoshida and Roman Anthony lead the lineup as designated hitter and leadoff, respectively, against the Brewers.
Boston's offense ranks low in runs and high in strikeouts, prompting changes to improve performance.

title: Cleveland radio host says Cavaliers are ‘scared’ of facing the Celtics in the playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers prefer the No. 4 playoff seed to avoid facing Boston Celtics in the second round.
Celtics have dominated the Cavaliers this season, sweeping the regular-season series and going 10-2 in recent games.
Cavaliers are dealing with injuries and uncertain lineups as they approach the postseason, according to ESPN's Andrew Goldhammer.

title: Where you live in Mass. can shape your electric bill — is yours getting out of control?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts residents can pay drastically different electricity rates depending on their location, with municipal utilities often costing less.
Municipal utilities serve 52 Mass. communities, offering cheaper rates due to easier maintenance and long-term contracts with nuclear plants.
Switching utility systems involves high costs; municipal aggregation is an alternative allowing towns to bulk purchase electricity.

title: Northeastern students rally over lack of communication after recent violence
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Northeastern students rallied over administrative communication lapses after fatal violence near campus.
Protesters criticized the university's delayed alerts and reliance on external news for safety updates.
Students and faculty demand better transparency on violent incidents, citing reliance on news for information.

title: BU president pauses signage policy after Pride flag controversy
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: BU President Melissa Gilliam pauses signage policy after protests over removal of Pride flags from campus.
The policy, part of BU's guidelines, prohibits outward-facing materials on university property, leading to controversy.
Gilliam emphasizes need for discussion on policy and community values, acknowledging LGBTQ+ impact from flag removals.

title: Red Sox complete Sonny Gray trade, send pitching prospect to Cardinals
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Red Sox complete trade, sending right-handed pitch prospect Patrick Galle to the Cardinals as the player to be named later.
Patrick Galle, 22, was drafted from Ole Miss and previously played for Single A Salem, with a 4.50 ERA in limited appearances.
Sonny Gray, acquired by the Red Sox in November, has a 4.50 ERA this season and is set to pitch against the Brewers.
